next_steps: Evaluate for underlying systemic inflammatory or autoimmune disease including Rheumatoid Factor, anti-CCP, and ANCA
Obtain inflammatory markers (ESR and CRP) to assess for systemic vasculitis
Review history for potential causes of severe B6 deficiency (e.g., malabsorption, medication interactions)
Perform a detailed physical exam focusing on joints and skin for evidence of vasculitic lesions
Consider nerve and muscle biopsy if symptoms persist despite B6 repletion and inflammatory workup is suggestive of vasculitis
Maintain management of urinary tract infection and optimize pain control while assessing neurologic recovery
